purpose: A growing source-backed database of high-value developer skills for teams using Claude Code. It is optimized for turning repeated front-end, back-end, data, testing, platform, security, and AI-assisted workflows into reusable skills that follow the official skill format.

name_rules: Use lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
Keep names at or below 64 characters
Do not include reserved words such as anthropic or claude
Prefer clear activity-oriented names over vague names like helper or utils
description_rules: State what the skill does and when to use it
Include concrete trigger terms developers naturally use
Keep it concise enough for reliable discovery
body_rules: Keep SKILL.md focused and move large references into supporting files
Reference support files directly from SKILL.md and keep references one level deep
Bundle scripts for deterministic checks, validation, or report generation
Use standing instructions rather than one-off task steps
Build evaluation scenarios and validator loops before expanding the skill

categories.frontend: UI architecture, component systems, accessibility, performance, and browser-facing workflows.
categories.backend: APIs, services, distributed systems, authentication, jobs, and production runtime concerns.
categories.data: Schema design, SQL quality, migrations, analytics events, pipelines, and search/indexing.
categories.testing: Unit, integration, E2E, regression, bug reproduction, and test feedback loops.
categories.platform: CI/CD, containers, infrastructure, monorepos, release workflows, and developer experience.
categories.security-reliability: Threat modeling, secret management, observability, resiliency, incidents, and recovery.
categories.ai-productivity: Skill extraction, conventions, PR review, agent orchestration, docs, and codebase intelligence.

skills.name: building-accessible-ui
skills.title: Accessible UI Review
skills.description: Reviews interface code for accessibility issues, semantic structure, keyboard behavior, focus management, and interaction risks. Use when building or reviewing component libraries, forms, dialogs, navigation, and responsive interfaces.
implementation_notes: Prefer deterministic checks where possible, then finish with manual interaction review.
Pair well with browser-based verification and component-level examples.

suggested_frontmatter.when_to_use: Use for React, Vue, HTML, CSS, form, modal, menu, table, and design-system tasks.

skills.name: designing-component-systems
skills.title: Component System Design
skills.description: Designs reusable UI component systems, prop APIs, composition rules, and state boundaries. Use when creating or refactoring design systems, shared UI packages, or component libraries.
implementation_notes: Keep rules opinionated and consistent across frameworks.
Include anti-patterns such as prop explosion and style leakage.

skills.name: debugging-react-rendering
skills.title: React Rendering Debugger
skills.description: Diagnoses unnecessary renders, stale closures, memoization mistakes, and state propagation issues in React applications. Use when UI feels slow, renders are noisy, or component updates are confusing.
implementation_notes: Focus on render cause tracing, dependency analysis, and before-after verification.

skills.name: hardening-forms-and-validation
skills.title: Form and Validation Hardening
skills.description: Strengthens form UX, validation rules, error states, async submission behavior, and client-server contract alignment. Use when shipping login, checkout, onboarding, profile, or settings forms.
implementation_notes: Cover disabled states, loading states, retry paths, and schema parity with the backend.

skills.name: modern-css-architecture
skills.title: Modern CSS Architecture
skills.description: Improves CSS architecture, token usage, layout consistency, and responsive styling strategy. Use when refactoring styling systems, Tailwind conventions, CSS modules, or shared UI foundations.
implementation_notes: Keep naming, spacing, and token rules short enough to load reliably.

skills.name: optimizing-bundle-performance
skills.title: Bundle Performance Optimizer
skills.description: Finds bundle growth, heavy dependencies, route-splitting opportunities, and hydration risks. Use when load time, bundle size, or interaction latency becomes a concern.
implementation_notes: Prefer measurable budgets and build output analysis over generic advice.

skills.name: verifying-responsive-layouts
skills.title: Responsive Layout Verifier
skills.description: Checks breakpoint behavior, overflow, spacing collapse, and layout resilience across screen sizes. Use when shipping pages, dashboards, marketing sites, or mobile-heavy workflows.
implementation_notes: Combine CSS inspection with browser-based viewport checks.

skills.name: reviewing-frontend-security
skills.title: Frontend Security Review
skills.description: Reviews browser-facing code for XSS, token exposure, unsafe rendering, insecure storage, and client-side trust mistakes. Use when handling user content, auth state, embeds, or rich text.
implementation_notes: Keep security guidance concrete and tied to actual UI code paths.

skills.name: designing-rest-apis
skills.title: REST API Design
skills.description: Designs REST APIs with clear resource boundaries, versioning rules, pagination, idempotency, and error contracts. Use when creating or refactoring HTTP services and public or internal APIs.
implementation_notes: Emphasize contract stability and smallest safe change.

skills.name: designing-graphql-apis
skills.title: GraphQL API Design
skills.description: Designs GraphQL schemas, resolver boundaries, batching, and authorization rules. Use when building graphs, federated services, or resolver-heavy integrations.
implementation_notes: Include explicit notes on batching, caching, and schema evolution.

skills.name: building-background-jobs
skills.title: Background Job Builder
skills.description: Designs reliable background jobs, retry logic, scheduling strategy, idempotency, and failure handling. Use when adding workers, task queues, cron-style jobs, or async processing pipelines.
implementation_notes: Push for explicit retry classes, deduplication, and observability hooks.

skills.name: implementing-auth-flows
skills.title: Authentication Flow Builder
skills.description: Implements authentication and authorization flows across sessions, tokens, roles, and privileged actions. Use when shipping login, SSO, OAuth, password reset, invitation, or RBAC features.
implementation_notes: Treat auth changes as high-risk and recommend validator loops and threat review.

skills.name: handling-webhooks
skills.title: Webhook Integration Hardening
skills.description: Builds and reviews webhook consumers for signature verification, retries, deduplication, ordering issues, and replay safety. Use when integrating payment, messaging, or external event providers.
implementation_notes: Center the skill on authenticity, retries, and safe reprocessing.

skills.name: reviewing-backend-concurrency
skills.title: Backend Concurrency Review
skills.description: Reviews concurrent backend code for race conditions, deadlocks, ordering bugs, lock contention, and inconsistent side effects. Use when services coordinate shared state, workers, or high-throughput workloads.
implementation_notes: Good fit for Go, Java, Python, .NET, and Node worker systems.

skills.name: building-event-driven-services
skills.title: Event-Driven Service Design
skills.description: Designs event-driven services with explicit contracts, delivery semantics, replay handling, and consumer isolation. Use when adopting queues, streams, outbox patterns, or domain events.
implementation_notes: Make delivery semantics and failure assumptions visible.

skills.name: reviewing-caching-strategies
skills.title: Caching Strategy Review
skills.description: Reviews cache key design, invalidation strategy, TTL choices, layering, and stale-read risks. Use when introducing Redis, CDN, query, or application-level caching.
implementation_notes: Prefer workload-specific guidance over generic caching advice.

skills.name: hardening-file-uploads
skills.title: File Upload Hardening
skills.description: Secures file upload flows for validation, scanning, storage isolation, content-type trust, and post-processing safety. Use when handling avatars, attachments, imports, or user-generated media.
implementation_notes: Treat parser execution and storage exposure as first-class concerns.

skills.name: documenting-api-contracts
skills.title: API Contract Documentation
skills.description: Produces concise API contract documentation, examples, and change notes that stay aligned with code. Use when shipping endpoints, SDKs, integrations, or internal platform surfaces.
implementation_notes: Pair especially well with schema-first or OpenAPI-first teams.

skills.name: designing-database-schemas
skills.title: Database Schema Designer
skills.description: Designs relational schemas, keys, constraints, and normalization tradeoffs for operational systems. Use when creating new tables, domain models, or storage-backed product features.
implementation_notes: Keep business invariants explicit in the schema guidance.

skills.name: reviewing-sql-performance
skills.title: SQL Performance Review
skills.description: Reviews SQL queries for plan quality, indexing opportunities, cardinality traps, and unnecessary scans. Use when queries slow down APIs, jobs, dashboards, or analytics pipelines.
implementation_notes: Require explain-plan thinking and measurable before-after checks.

skills.name: planning-data-migrations
skills.title: Data Migration Planner
skills.description: Plans safe schema and data migrations with backfills, rollout sequencing, rollback paths, and runtime compatibility checks. Use when changing live data models or moving data between stores.
implementation_notes: Favor plan-validate-execute workflows and explicit rollback points.

skills.name: designing-search-and-indexes
skills.title: Search and Index Design
skills.description: Designs search schemas, indexing strategies, ranking tradeoffs, and query filters. Use when implementing application search, faceting, vector or lexical retrieval, or hybrid discovery features.
implementation_notes: Cover query intent, recall, ranking, and freshness separately.

skills.name: modeling-analytics-events
skills.title: Analytics Event Modeling
skills.description: Models analytics events, naming conventions, user properties, and event contracts for trustworthy product analysis. Use when adding instrumentation to frontend or backend product flows.
implementation_notes: Stress consistency, versioning, and privacy-aware field selection.

skills.name: validating-etl-pipelines
skills.title: ETL Pipeline Validator
skills.description: Validates ETL and ELT pipelines for freshness, schema drift, lineage breaks, duplication, and reconciliation errors. Use when building ingestion jobs, warehouse transforms, or sync systems.
implementation_notes: Use machine-verifiable checks where possible and preserve sample failure diagnostics.

skills.name: generating-unit-tests
skills.title: Unit Test Generator
skills.description: Generates focused unit tests around branches, edge cases, and regressions without overfitting to implementation details. Use when extending logic, fixing bugs, or improving confidence in isolated modules.
implementation_notes: Bias toward behavior-focused tests and small deterministic fixtures.

skills.name: designing-integration-tests
skills.title: Integration Test Designer
skills.description: Designs integration tests around service contracts, persistence, side effects, and environment setup. Use when changes cross module, network, storage, or framework boundaries.
implementation_notes: Use realistic dependencies but keep setup repeatable and observable.

skills.name: stabilizing-e2e-tests
skills.title: E2E Test Stabilizer
skills.description: Improves end-to-end tests by removing flakiness, clarifying waits, and aligning assertions with user-visible outcomes. Use when browser tests are brittle or slow.
implementation_notes: Lean on deterministic selectors, network awareness, and state setup shortcuts.

skills.name: reviewing-test-coverage
skills.title: Coverage Gap Reviewer
skills.description: Reviews what is not tested and prioritizes the highest-risk missing scenarios. Use when shipping new code, after large refactors, or after incidents that revealed blind spots.
implementation_notes: Focus on business risk and failure probability, not raw percentages alone.

skills.name: snapshot-regression-checks
skills.title: Snapshot Regression Checks
skills.description: Applies snapshot and golden-file testing carefully for stable outputs such as APIs, emails, rendered templates, and serialization. Use when outputs should remain consistent over time.
implementation_notes: Include criteria for when snapshots are appropriate and when they are noisy.

skills.name: reproducing-bugs-from-logs
skills.title: Bug Reproduction From Logs
skills.description: Turns logs, traces, and error reports into concrete reproduction steps and regression tests. Use when investigating production failures, support tickets, or intermittent defects.
implementation_notes: Start from raw evidence and preserve the minimal failing case.

skills.name: hardening-ci-pipelines
skills.title: CI Pipeline Hardening
skills.description: Improves CI pipelines for speed, reliability, caching, matrix strategy, and failure isolation. Use when builds are slow, flaky, expensive, or hard to trust.
implementation_notes: Push for fast feedback loops and clear quality gates.

skills.name: shipping-containerized-services
skills.title: Containerized Service Shipping
skills.description: Packages services for containers with lean images, safe defaults, environment strategy, and deployment readiness checks. Use when containerizing APIs, workers, or internal tools.
implementation_notes: Cover build caching, non-root execution, and health checks.

skills.name: managing-infrastructure-as-code
skills.title: Infrastructure as Code Review
skills.description: Reviews infrastructure code for modularity, environment separation, drift risk, secret handling, and safe rollout patterns. Use when working with Terraform, Pulumi, or cloud templates.
implementation_notes: Encourage explicit change plans and state safety.

skills.name: improving-developer-experience
skills.title: Developer Experience Optimizer
skills.description: Improves local setup, scripts, docs, task runners, and onboarding paths for faster developer flow. Use when repos are hard to bootstrap, inconsistent, or slow to work in.
implementation_notes: Value clear defaults, one-command setup, and smaller cognitive load.

skills.name: maintaining-monorepos
skills.title: Monorepo Maintenance
skills.description: Maintains monorepos through workspace boundaries, task graph design, ownership rules, and incremental validation. Use when scaling packages, apps, services, and shared libraries together.
implementation_notes: Keep guidance aligned with dependency direction and validation scope.

skills.name: running-release-checklists
skills.title: Release Checklist Runner
skills.description: Runs release preparation checklists covering quality gates, migrations, rollback readiness, communication, and post-release verification. Use when preparing staging or production releases.
implementation_notes: Best as a workflow skill with explicit validator steps.

skills.name: threat-modeling-features
skills.title: Feature Threat Modeling
skills.description: Threat-models new features by identifying assets, trust boundaries, attack paths, and mitigations. Use when reviewing auth changes, payments, admin tools, integrations, or other sensitive workflows.
implementation_notes: Keep the method lightweight enough for regular use but concrete enough to drive mitigations.

skills.name: auditing-secrets-and-config
skills.title: Secrets and Config Audit
skills.description: Audits secret handling, environment configuration, rotation practices, and accidental exposure risks. Use when reviewing repositories, deployment configs, CI, or incident follow-up.
implementation_notes: Focus on removal of trust assumptions and safer defaults.

skills.name: improving-observability
skills.title: Observability Improvement
skills.description: Improves logging, metrics, tracing, and diagnostic context for faster debugging and healthier operations. Use when incidents are hard to diagnose or system behavior is opaque.
implementation_notes: Tie every recommendation to a concrete debugging or alerting outcome.

skills.name: setting-slos-and-alerts
skills.title: SLO and Alert Design
skills.description: Designs service-level objectives, indicators, and actionable alerts that reduce noise and improve operational focus. Use when tuning monitoring for APIs, background systems, or customer-facing journeys.
implementation_notes: Prefer user-impact signals over infrastructure vanity metrics.

skills.name: reviewing-failure-modes
skills.title: Failure Mode Review
skills.description: Reviews how systems fail under dependency outages, partial writes, retries, and degraded states. Use when services coordinate remote calls, queues, or critical transactions.
implementation_notes: Center on what users experience and what operators can recover quickly.

skills.name: validating-backup-and-restore
skills.title: Backup and Restore Validation
skills.description: Validates backup scope, recovery steps, restore drills, and data integrity assumptions. Use when services store critical operational or customer data.
implementation_notes: Treat proof of restoration as more important than backup existence.

skills.name: extracting-reusable-skills
skills.title: Reusable Skill Extractor
skills.description: Turns repeated successful workflows into reusable skills with proper names, descriptions, support files, and evaluation ideas. Use when recurring tasks reveal stable patterns worth codifying.
implementation_notes: This is the meta-skill that should feed the database over time.

skills.name: curating-team-conventions
skills.title: Team Convention Curator
skills.description: Curates coding conventions, architectural defaults, and review standards into structured reference material that can later become skills. Use when teams repeat style, design, or workflow guidance.
implementation_notes: Keep conventions opinionated, testable, and easy to locate by domain.

skills.name: generating-pr-reviews
skills.title: Pull Request Review Assistant
skills.description: Reviews pull requests for correctness, risk, test gaps, migration impact, and maintainability. Use when preparing PR feedback, self-reviewing changes, or training reviewers.
implementation_notes: Best when paired with explicit change summaries and validation commands.

skills.name: planning-multi-agent-work
skills.title: Multi-Agent Work Planner
skills.description: Plans work decomposition, task boundaries, handoffs, and validation points for multi-agent development workflows. Use when parallelizing large refactors, research, or incident investigations.
implementation_notes: Keep sub-tasks independent and route large evidence into files.

skills.name: building-rag-ready-docs
skills.title: RAG-Ready Documentation Builder
skills.description: Restructures documentation for retrieval quality with chunk-friendly sections, explicit metadata, and stable terminology. Use when preparing codebase docs, runbooks, or API guides for AI systems.
implementation_notes: Focus on consistent headings, examples, and low-ambiguity naming.

skills.name: turning-runbooks-into-skills
skills.title: Runbook to Skill Converter
skills.description: Converts operational runbooks into reusable skills with standing instructions, validator loops, and safe invocation controls. Use when a manual checklist is repeated often enough to automate guidance.
implementation_notes: Strong fit for deployment, incident, and compliance workflows.

skills.name: cataloging-codebase-patterns
skills.title: Codebase Pattern Cataloger
skills.description: Catalogs recurring codebase patterns, preferred implementations, and anti-patterns so future skills and prompts can reference real repository behavior. Use when onboarding to large codebases or consolidating conventions.
implementation_notes: This helps keep skills grounded in actual code rather than generic advice.

skills.name: evaluating-tooling-choices
skills.title: Engineering Tooling Evaluator
skills.description: Evaluates libraries, frameworks, and platform choices using adoption fit, migration cost, risk, and operational burden. Use when deciding between tools for frontend, backend, testing, or infrastructure work.
implementation_notes: Keep tradeoffs explicit and tied to team constraints rather than hype.

maintenance_policy.selection_rule: Prefer official documentation first, then active public repositories with clear structure, then public community tips that are specific and repeatable.
maintenance_policy.update_rule: Add or modify entries only when at least one strong source supports the pattern and the description can clearly state what the skill does and when to use it.
maintenance_policy.quality_rule: Keep names precise, descriptions discovery-friendly, and support files one level deep from SKILL.md.
